title: Project overview
content: The project framework combines a multimodal urban resource pool, a spatiotemporal pattern engine, and an AI decision service for Chunxi Road.
recommendations: Use the signal grid as the master table, then join road, OD, and video features into the same spatial frame.
Treat Mamba as the long-sequence backbone and Transformer as the cross-modal association layer.

title: Signal activity peaks
content: The hourly signal panel spans 168 hours across 90 grids. The strongest weekly peak occurs at 2025-03-22 18:00 with 354,429 signals.
recommendations: Prioritize crowd operations and tactical retail programming during the late afternoon and evening surge.
Use hourly rolling forecasts to flag overload windows for the central hotspot cells.

title: Dual-camera fused temporal pattern
content: KFC and RF are only labels for the two camera angles. The fused ALL stream peaks at 2026-03-29 15:12 with 17,196 detections across both views, and the shared heatmap is the main movement evidence layer.
recommendations: Use the fused ALL timeline and heatmap as the primary display and analysis layer for the video module.
Treat per-camera series as diagnostic support rather than the main narrative for the site.

title: Hotspot structure
content: The top hotspot cell is 3_1 with 2,540,720 weekly signals, showing how activity concentrates along the Chunxi Road and Taikoo Li spine.
recommendations: Use hotspot ranking for storefront sequencing, pedestrian comfort upgrades, and public-space monitoring.
Cross-check hotspot cells against subway access and crossing density before designing interventions.

title: Multimodal forecast comparison
content: The multimodal forecast bundle compares Transformer+Mamba, Transformer-only, and Mamba-only models over 22 test hours. The current lowest test Total MAE comes from Transformer-only, while the hybrid branch assigns 80.0% of its gate weight to dynamic signals and 12.4% to video priors.
recommendations: Use the hybrid output for multimodal interpretation, but benchmark deployment choices against the model-comparison panel rather than assuming the mixed branch always wins.
Prioritize adding denser video, street-view, and better-timed social features if the goal is to beat the current signal-dominant forecast baseline.

title: OD corridors
content: The strongest weekly OD corridor is 3_1 to 4_1 with 196,306 movements after filtering to hourly rows.
recommendations: Focus wayfinding and queue relief on adjacent-cell exchanges rather than only on long-distance flows.
Treat the OD file carefully because it mixes hourly and daily rows; filter by aggregation_level first.

title: Road and subway context
content: The static context layer contains 534 road lines, 154 pedestrian ways, 20 crossings, and 5 subway entrances.
recommendations: Engineer these layers into per-grid features such as crossing count, pedestrian density, and subway proximity.
Use the static layer as explanatory context, not as the only modeling input.

title: Public perception and street image
content: The public-perception workbook currently holds 61 curated records, while the street-scene pilot includes 4 scored images with an average beauty score of 75.0.
recommendations: Use text and street-scene data as qualitative enrichment until their spatial and temporal alignment is expanded.
Add precise geocoding and capture time to make these layers more useful for Transformer-style multimodal fusion.

title: Business and planning recommendations
content: The dashboard supports stakeholder-specific guidance: residents need congestion-aware route advice, businesses need footfall timing and corridor ranking, and planners need hotspot, accessibility, and resilience evidence.
recommendations: For businesses: align promotions, staffing, and storefront activations with evening and adjacent-cell corridor peaks.
For planners: combine hotspot and crossing density maps to target pedestrian safety and comfort upgrades.
For operators: prepare rainy-day shelter and queue strategies because activity remains high even under wet conditions.
